channel.title: Gen Z on MinWeb
channel.description: Recent content in Gen Z on MinWeb

item.title: Why Gen Z Is Sabotaging Corporate AI Rollouts
item.description:
If you feel like your workplace has turned into a digital battlefield, you aren’t imagining things. Walk into any office or skim the Slack channels of a major corporation, and you’ll find a mess. Executives are shoving AI down everyone’s throats, while employees are either ignoring it, breaking it, or just plain exhausted by the whole charade.

Headlines are currently buzzing with a provocative claim: Gen Z workers are sabotaging AI rollouts because they fear being replaced. A 2026 report from Writer and Workplace Intelligence found that 44% of Gen Z workers admit to actively undermining their company’s AI strategy.

item.title: The $5 Billion Analog Rebellion: Why Gen Z Is Going Offline
item.description:
Does your smart refrigerator ever stop working because it needs a software update? Does your daily screen time report make you want to throw your phone into a lake? You aren’t alone.

A strange, tactile rebellion is happening right under our noses. This isn’t just nostalgia or a trendy aesthetic choice. It’s a fundamental shift in how people are choosing to interact with the world. While digital companies scramble to monetize every millisecond of your attention, a massive demographic is quietly pulling the plug. (Honestly, I’m right there with them—my flip phone is the best purchase I’ve made all year.)
